description: The Cotroni crime family was an Italian Canadian crime family based in Montreal, Quebec, established in the 1940s by Vincenzo Cotroni. It was considered a branch of the Bonanno crime family of New York City. The family controlled territory in southern Quebec and Ontario, engaging in racketeering, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. An internal war in the late 1970s led to the rise of the Sicilian Rizzuto faction, and the family became defunct by the 2000s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Frank Cotroni was sentenced to seven years in prison after he and his son pleaded guilty to plotting to import 180 kilograms of cocaine from Colombia.
country_links.context: Frank Cotroni and his son pleaded guilty to plotting to import cocaine from Colombia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The recordings of an oblivious Violi revealed much about the operations of the Mafia in not only Canada, but also the U.S. and Italy as well.
country_links.context: The Cotroni family's operations were discussed in Italy, and a police bug revealed information about the Mafia in Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: Galante planned to make Montreal a pivotal location in the importation of heroin from overseas for distribution in New York and across the United States via the French Connection.
country_links.context: The Cotroni family formed a strong connection to the Bonanno crime family in New York, and heroin was distributed in New York and across the United States via the French Connection.
